metadata.title: Why the pipeline runs the same crash three times before it believes it
metadata.purpose: ASAN detects real memory errors deterministically; randomized allocators and timing artifacts crash inconsistently, so requiring 3/3 filters true bugs from noise before anything is reported.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: allocator races; cgroups/RLIMIT enforcement; OOM killer
metadata.visual_object: Three parallel containers each emitting ✓/✗; a gate downstream that opens only on ✓✓✓.
metadata.hook: A single crash isn't trusted — the same PoC runs in three isolated containers and advances only if all three abort.

beats.narration_text: A crafted WAV triggers a heap OOB write. The grader runs it three times; all three ASAN-abort identically → advances. A fuzzer artifact that crashes once due to malloc randomization → 1/3 → discarded.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If you run an ASAN binary three times on identical input, will a real corruption bug crash all three, while timing-dependent false positives crash only once or twice?
A single crash isn't trusted — the same PoC runs in three isolated containers and advances only if all three abort.
ASAN detects real memory errors deterministically; randomized allocators and timing artifacts crash inconsistently, so requiring 3/3 filters true bugs from noise before anything is reported.
beats.visual: Three parallel containers each emitting ✓/✗; a gate downstream that opens only on ✓✓✓.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: Crafted PNG integer-overflow alloc. Runs A/B/C all heap-buffer-overflow → 3/3 advance. Contrast: malloc-state-dependent artifact → ✓✗✓ → 1/3 discarded.
ASAN detects real memory errors deterministically; randomized allocators and timing artifacts crash inconsistently, so requiring 3/3 filters true bugs from noise before anything is reported.
